repo_prior_art.cite: ThmSmith/doc/API.md §3 PartialID is seed/empty; closest banked pid_network_exposure_v1, pid_dose_response_shape_v1, and pid_long_term_surrogate_v1 target graph completion, shape-restricted dose response, and surrogate drift, not learned-representation distortion

published_prior_art.cite: ManskiTamer2002 and BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011/2012: generic interval/random-set sharp-region machinery; no selector semantics for text/image oracle representations

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue ThmSmith proposal states a Borel epsilon-fiber envelope for learned text/image treatment representations

published_prior_art.cite: BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2012: random-set partial identification gives the closest general selection-envelope machinery; representation-specific selector target is not stated

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue ThmSmith proposal states a fiber-invariance obstruction for a GenAI learned-representation plug-in endpoint

published_prior_art.cite: ImaiNakamura2024TextTreatments gives the GenAI representation workflow; FongGrimmer2023 and EgamiFongGrimmerRobertsStewart2022 warn about latent/text representation identification, but do not state this epsilon-fiber-invariance frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue ThmSmith proposal contains this representation-distortion singleton diagnostic

published_prior_art.cite: none specific; this is routine interval oscillation algebra attached to the proposed envelope

novelty_flags.label: proposal
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the main envelope is close to standard random-set/selector partial-ID algebra, so flagship status needs a stronger named GenAI endpoint theorem or broader generic-class result beyond the current interval frontier.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2 / Theorem 2 positioning
soundness_flags.one_line: The proposal sometimes treats epsilon-fiber invariance as a necessary-and-sufficient certificate for the published plug-in endpoint, but Theorem 2 only makes B_epsilon a singleton; equality to Delta_plug also needs endpoint calibration of mu-dagger to the fiber constants.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add an explicit endpoint-calibration condition, e.g. mu-dagger(r_j) equals the common fiber value when fiber invariance holds, and separate singleton-envelope validity from plug-in-endpoint validity.
Upgrade Conjecture 2 into a necessary-and-sufficient uniform validity theorem for the named Imai-Nakamura/GPI fitted representation-surface endpoint over an open class of observable laws.

Add one sharp boundary corollary quantifying how the envelope diameter grows with fiber oscillations and when sign decisions are robust; this would make the applied contribution less like a relabelled random-set interval.

recommended_next_step: Revise around the tier gap: patch the plug-in endpoint coherence issue and strengthen the GenAI endpoint obstruction enough to plausibly clear the flagship floor.
